title: taraxacum officinale subsp. laevigatum
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. laevigatum, commonly known as the smooth dandelion, is a subspecies of the dandelion plant, characterized by its smooth leaves and stems. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum officinale subsp. laevigatum lie in its leaves, roots, and flowers, which are rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals such as potassium and iron. This plant is known for its health benefits, including its ability to support liver and kidney function, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Taraxacum officinale subsp. laevigatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used fresh in salads or cooked as a vegetable.
